DISCOVERY 302, THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORORATION MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS FROM PLTF PASSION WILSON IS GRANTED IN PART. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS THAT REQUESTS 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, AND 10 PURPORT TO REQUIRE PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS THAT ARE PROTECTED BY THE ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT AND ATTORNEY CLIENT PRIVILEGE IS NOT WITHOUT MERIT. HOWEVER, MANY NON-PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS ALSO FALL WITHIN THESE CATEGORIES OF DOCUMENTS AND SHOULD HAVE BEEN PRODUCED. MOREOVER, REQUETS 8, 9, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, AND 17 DO NOT CALL FOR PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS. WITHIN 30 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER PLAINTIFF SHALL SERVE VERIFIED CODE-COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION'S FIRST SET OF REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION, AND PRODUCE ALL RESPONSIVE NON-PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS NO LATER THAN 45 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER ACCOMPANIED BY A PRIVILEGE LOG. ORDER SIGNED. ERNST HALPERIN, JUDGE PRO-TEM, JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, CLERK, NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents; From Plaintiff Amanda Burks is GRANTED IN PART.
